relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks about flows from the body.
A male discharge creates a ritual state.
The discharge itself defines the uncleanness.
Bed and seat receive the contact state.
Touching the bed requires washing and waiting.
Sitting where he sat requires washing and waiting.
Touching his flesh requires washing and waiting.
Spittle contact also requires washing and waiting.
The saddle carries the state.
Contact with what was under him requires washing and waiting.
Unwashed-hand contact requires washing and waiting.
Earthen and wooden vessels are handled differently.
When the discharge ends, seven days and living water restore cleanness.
On the eighth day birds are brought to the priest.
Covering is made from the discharge.
Emission of seed brings washing and evening uncleanness.
Garments touched by seed are washed and wait.
Both partners wash and wait after emission.
Menstrual blood creates a seven-day ritual state.
Bed and seat receive the menstrual contact state.
Touching her bed requires washing and waiting.
Touching her seat requires washing and waiting.
Contact with bed or vessel lasts until evening.
Lying with her during menstruation shares the ritual state.
Prolonged blood flow is treated with the same ritual seriousness.
Her bed and seat carry the state during the flow.
Contact requires washing and waiting.
When the flow ends, she counts seven days.
On the eighth day she brings birds to the priest.
Covering is made, and she returns clean before YHWH.
The sanctuary in the midst is protected by ritual boundaries.
This is instruction for discharge and seed emission.
It includes menstruation, male and female flows, and shared contact.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 15 is body-honoring when read rightly: flows are not shame, sex is not evil, menstruation is not moral failure. Washing, waiting, and return protect the holy without degrading the body.
